title: Latched Quick Chat — Semantic Search Golden Record
description: One record per labeled test question. The eval runner posts `question` to the semantic-search Edge Function and compares the response to `expected_matches` (plus optional decomposition / tier / no-match assertions).

id.description: Stable test ID. Never reused — append-only so failures stay diffable across runs.

question.description: Raw user question as it would arrive from the client. Verbatim — keep typos, casing, punctuation as a real user would type.

test_type.description: Slice tag — used to compute per-slice metrics (paraphrase accuracy, off-topic rejection rate, bras downgrade compliance, etc.).

category.description: FAQ category this question targets (matches `faq_entries.category`). Null for compound / off_topic. Used for category-level slicing.

expected_matches.description: Ordered list of FAQ matches the response should contain. Empty array means the response should be `{ matched: false }` (off-topic, bras_downgrade, low_confidence).

tier.description: Expected confidence_tier. LOW is never expected in a returned match — LOW results are filtered out before response.

expect_escalation.description: If true, the response item must include escalation_text.

expected_sub_questions.description: For compound questions: the sub-questions the decomposer should produce (semantic match, not exact string). Length should equal expected_matches length.

anatomy_context.description: Optional user_profile context to inject before calling the function. Used to test that anatomy/pump context shapes decomposition correctly.

expected_contradiction.description: For compound questions only. true = response should include a contradiction_warning. false = warning should be null. null = don't assert.

notes.description: Why this case exists — failure mode it guards against, ticket link, IBCLC review note.
